# TASK-2: Redesign System with Brownish/Warm Color Palette

## Context
Revamp the entire site's visual system to prioritize readability with a warm, brownish color palette inspired by Monokai and Claude design languages. This includes updating color variables, typography hierarchy, spacing refinements, and contrast improvements across the global theme (light and dark modes). The goal is to create a cohesive, pleasant design that reduces eye strain while maintaining visual hierarchy and accessibility.

## References
- **Agent**: [code-generator](../agents/code-generator.agent.md)
- **Instructions**:
  - [style](../instructions/style.instructions.md)

## Dependencies
None

## Steps
1. [ ] **Extract reference color palettes**: Document the specific colors from Monokai and Claude themes (background, text, accents, muted tones). Create a color swatches reference in project notes.

2. [ ] **Update CSS custom properties in `app/globals.css`**:
   - Replace cool blue tones with warm browns, taupes, and earthy accents
   - Define new variables: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--surface`, `--accent` (warm tone), `--accent-hover`, `--border`, `--foreground-muted`
   - Update both `:root` (light mode) and `.dark` (dark mode) color scopes
   - Ensure sufficient contrast ratios (WCAG AA minimum: 4.5:1 for text)

3. [ ] **Refine typography for readability**:
   - Review and adjust font sizes, line heights, and letter spacing in global styles
   - Ensure code blocks and monospace text in `CodeBlock.tsx` use appropriate contrast against warm backgrounds
   - Test readability across different screen sizes

4. [ ] **Update component-specific styles** (if needed):
   - Check `Sidebar.tsx`, `PageHeader.tsx`, `Callout.tsx` for hardcoded colors and replace with CSS variables
   - Ensure hover/focus states work well with the new warm palette
   - Update `ThemeToggle.tsx` to reflect the new light/dark theme labels if appropriate

5. [ ] **Verify spacing and contrast**:
   - Test visual hierarchy: headings, body text, and muted text are clearly differentiated
   - Ensure borders and dividers are visible but subtle against warm backgrounds
   - Check that focus rings and interactive elements provide clear visual feedback

6. [ ] **Test across the site**:
   - Manually review all documentation pages in light and dark modes
   - Test code blocks, callouts, and other special components
   - Verify the theme toggle switches between light/dark correctly
   - Check landing page (`app/page.tsx`) and key documentation sections

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global color palette in `app/globals.css` updated with warm, brownish tones inspired by Monokai/Claude
- [ ] Both light and dark mode color variables are defined and harmonious
- [ ] All text meets WCAG AA contrast requirements (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
- [ ] Typography hierarchy (headings, body, muted) is clearly preserved and enhanced for readability
- [ ] No hardcoded colors remain in component files; all use CSS variables
- [ ] Theme toggle works and switches between light/dark modes visually
- [ ] Manual visual review confirms improved readability and cohesive warm aesthetic across all pages
- [ ] No regressions in layout, spacing, or component functionality

## Notes
- Monokai theme reference: Warm background (#272822), accent oranges/yellows (#E6DB74, #FD971F), muted text (#75715E)
- Claude design language: Warm grays, subtle browns, accessible contrast
- Prioritize readability and eye comfort in all design decisions
- Consider creating a design system file documenting the new palette for future consistency
